industries.name: Electronics & Data Storage
uses: Ruthenium thin films as diffusion barrier and seed layer in copper dual-damascene interconnects at sub-7nm semiconductor nodes — ruthenium's low resistivity and resistance to copper diffusion make it a candidate to replace cobalt liners at the most advanced technology nodes

Giant magnetoresistance (GMR) read heads in hard disk drives use ruthenium spacer layers in the spin-valve structure — a 0.8nm Ru layer between cobalt-iron magnetic layers creates antiferromagnetic coupling essential to the GMR effect that enabled massive increases in HDD storage density
Resistive RAM (ReRAM) and phase-change memory cells use ruthenium oxide (RuO₂) electrodes for non-volatile memory in embedded applications

Ruthenium-based electrode coatings (RuO₂/TiO₂ mixed oxide, dimensionally stable anodes, DSA) for chlor-alkali electrolysis cells — the standard anode coating for industrial chlorine production replacing graphite anodes; also used in electrochlorination and electroplating

companies.name: Seagate Technology
companies.use: World's largest hard disk drive manufacturer. Major consumer of ruthenium for GMR and TMR read head spin-valve structures. Each HDD contains nanogram-scale ruthenium layers in the read sensor stack across billions of drives produced annually.

companies.name: Western Digital
companies.use: Major HDD and NAND flash manufacturer. Uses ruthenium in spin-valve GMR/TMR read head structures for hard drives. Also developing ruthenium-based electrode materials for next-generation 3D NAND and DRAM memory.

companies.name: Heraeus
companies.use: Major precious metal refiner and ruthenium product manufacturer. Produces ruthenium sputtering targets for HDD and semiconductor applications, ruthenium plating solutions, and ruthenium catalysts for chemical synthesis.

industries.name: Catalysts & Chemical Synthesis
uses: Ruthenium-based ammonia synthesis catalysts: newer Ru/carbon catalysts are 10–20× more active than traditional iron catalysts at lower temperature and pressure, enabling smaller-scale, distributed green ammonia production from renewable hydrogen

Grubbs ruthenium carbene catalysts (Grubbs 1st and 2nd generation) for olefin metathesis — Nobel Prize 2005 (Chauvin, Grubbs, Schrock). Used in pharmaceutical synthesis (macrolide antibiotics), polymer chemistry (ROMP), and specialty chemical production
Ruthenium tetroxide (RuO₄) as oxidant for dihydroxylation of alkenes and oxidation of primary and secondary alcohols in fine chemical and pharmaceutical synthesis
Ru/Al₂O₃ catalysts for hydrogenation of benzene to cyclohexane (nylon intermediate) and for Fischer-Tropsch synthesis of long-chain hydrocarbons from syngas in gas-to-liquids processes

companies.name: Johnson Matthey
companies.share: ~25% of platinum group metal catalyst market
companies.use: Major catalyst manufacturer and platinum group metal refiner. Produces ruthenium-based catalysts for ammonia synthesis, hydrogenation, and fine chemical applications. Also refines and recycles ruthenium from industrial spent catalysts.

companies.name: Evonik Industries
companies.use: Produces ruthenium-based homogeneous catalysts for pharmaceutical and specialty chemical synthesis. Supplies Grubbs-type Ru carbene catalysts under the Umicore-licensed HOVEYDA catalyst brand for industrial-scale olefin metathesis.

industries.name: Platinum Alloys & Jewelry
uses: Ruthenium hardens platinum and palladium jewelry alloys — Pt-Ru (3.5–5% Ru) and Pd-Ru alloys provide improved scratch resistance and hardness for fine jewelry while maintaining the white color and hypoallergenic properties of platinum group metals

Ruthenium alloyed with titanium (Ti-0.1%Ru) dramatically improves corrosion resistance of titanium in reducing acid environments — used in chemical plant equipment handling hydrochloric and sulfuric acids

Electrical contacts: ruthenium in gold and palladium contact alloys for low-force electrical connectors, relay contacts, and potentiometer tracks — ruthenium reduces contact wear and maintains low contact resistance
Fountain pen nibs tipped with ruthenium alloy — more durable than traditional osmium-iridium tips

companies.name: Tanaka Kikinzoku Kogyo
companies.use: Major precious metal products company producing ruthenium alloys for electrical contacts, jewelry, and electronic applications. Also produces ruthenium sputtering targets and plating solutions for HDD and semiconductor industries.

companies.name: Umicore
companies.use: Major platinum group metal refiner recovering ruthenium from secondary sources (spent catalysts, electronic scrap). Produces refined ruthenium metal, ruthenium chemicals, and ruthenium-containing electrode coatings for chlor-alkali.
